oligos.name: Oligo-dT30VN RT primer
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer; anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail and adds a 3' PCR handle to the cDNA
oligos.notes: Documents give the name 'oligodT30VN' / 'oligo(dT)' and use it at 0.125-0.5 uM. The 5' handle (ACGAGCATCAGCAGCATACGA) is the documented Smart-seq3 reverse PCR primer, which must bind the handle this primer installs (standard Smart-seq3 design); T30 = 30 dT, V = A/C/G, N = any base (anchor).

oligos.name: Smart-seq3 (original) template-switching oligo
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo; adds 5' recognition tag + UMI + rGrGrG to the cDNA 5' end during RT
oligos.notes: Printed as 5'-Biotin-AGAGACAGATTGCGCAATGNNNNNNNNrGrGrG-3' (IDT); 5' biotin; NNNNNNNN = 8-nt UMI; rGrGrG (riboguanosines) folded to GGG. Used at 0.75-2 uM. ATTGCGCAATG is the 5' recognition tag detected by zUMIs.

oligos.name: Smart-seq3xpress improved template-switching oligo
oligos.role: Improved TSO with a 2-nt W spacer to suppress strand-invasion/mis-priming while retaining UMI counting
oligos.notes: Printed as 5'-Biotin-AGAGACAGATTGCGCAATGNNNNNNNNWWrGrGrG-3' (IDT); 5' biotin; NNNNNNNN = 8-nt UMI; WW = two 'W' bases (each A or T) spacer between UMI and rGrGrG; rGrGrG folded to GGG. This is the recommended Smart-seq3xpress TSO.

oligos.name: Smart-seq3 forward primer
oligos.role: Pre-amplification PCR forward primer; targets the TSO tag and installs the Nextera s5/ME (i5-side) handle at the 5' end
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGATTGCGCAATG-3' (IDT). TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAG = Nextera s5 handle; AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = Tn5 mosaic end (ME); ATTGCGCAATG = TSO recognition tag. Used at 0.5-1 uM; must outcompete the TSO in PCR to avoid UMI inflation.

oligos.name: Smart-seq3 reverse primer
oligos.role: Pre-amplification PCR reverse primer; binds the oligo-dT handle at the cDNA 3' end
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-ACGAGCATCAGCAGCATACGA-3' (IDT). Used at 0.1-1 uM. Reverse complement of the oligo-dT primer 5' handle.

oligos.name: Custom Nextera i5 (index2) index PCR primer
oligos.role: Index PCR primer; binds the s5 handle and installs P5 + 10-bp i5 index
oligos.notes: Documents state 'custom Nextera index primers (0.5 uM) carrying 10-bp dual indexes'; the exact 10-bp barcodes are not tabulated. Sequence shown uses the standard Illumina P5 + Nextera s5 handles flanking the 10-bp i5 index. Amplified with Phusion, 12-14 cycles.

oligos.name: Custom Nextera i7 (index1) index PCR primer
oligos.role: Index PCR primer; binds the tagmentation ME/s7 handle and installs P7 + 10-bp i7 index
oligos.notes: Part of the custom Nextera dual-index primer pair (0.5 uM). Exact 10-bp i7 barcodes not tabulated in the documents; standard Illumina P7 + Nextera s7 handles flank the index. For MGI G400RS runs, phosphorylated index primers were used.

oligos.name: Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 1 (custom, used on MGI; equivalent to Nextera Read1)
oligos.notes: Printed as Read 1 custom primer 5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'. Reads the 5' recognition tag + UMI + GGG + cDNA for UMI-containing (5') molecules.

oligos.name: Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.notes: Printed as Read 2 custom primer 5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'. Nextera s7 + ME; reads internal cDNA from the tagmented (i7) end.

oligos.name: i7 index sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom index-read primer for the i7 (index1) index
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-CCGTATCATTCAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT-3'; used for MGI G400RS custom sequencing.

oligos.name: i5 index sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom index-read primer for the i5 (index2) index
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA-3'; reverse complement of the Read1 s5+ME handle; used for MGI G400RS custom sequencing.

oligos.name: MDA custom sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom sequencing primer (MDA read) for MGI G400RS
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-CGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTGAATGATACGGCGAC-3'; listed among custom MGI sequencing primers.

oligos.name: MGI rolling-circle amplification primer
oligos.role: Custom rolling-circle amplification primer for DNA nanoball (DNB) making on MGI
oligos.notes: Printed 5'-TCGCCGTATCATTCAAGCAGAAGACG-3'; used to make DNBs from circularized ssDNA for MGI DNBSEQ G400RS sequencing.

final_library.source_label: Smart-seq3xpress tagmented dual-index Nextera library (5'-UMI fragment shown)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [i5:10] TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAG AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ATTGCGCAATG [UMI:8] GGG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT CCGAGCCCACGAGAC [i7:10] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAG = Nextera s5 handle (Read 1 primer site)
CCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Nextera s7 handle reverse complement (Read 2 primer site)
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG = P7 reverse complement (Illumina flow-cell adapter)
NOTE: only 5'-most fragments carry the s5-tag-UMI-GGG block; internal tagmentation fragments have ME/s5 handles at both ends and contain no UMI.

evidence.locator: Smart-seq3xpress tagmented dual-index Nextera library (5'-UMI fragment shown)

library_generation.title: Single-cell sort & lysis
library_generation.summary: A single cell is FACS-sorted into ~300 nl lysis buffer under a 3 µl hydrophobic overlay and heat-denatured.
library_generation.note: Lysis buffer contains 0.1% Triton X-100, 5% PEG8000, oligo-dT30VN (0.125-0.5 uM), dNTPs and RNase inhibitor. The overlay (e.g. Vapor-Lock) both prevents evaporation of the nanoliter reaction and acts as a landing cushion for the sorted cell. Denature 72 °C, 10 min.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription + template switching
library_generation.summary: Maxima H Minus RT extends oligo-dT and, at the 5' end, switches to the TSO to append tag + UMI + GGG.
library_generation.note: RT at 42 °C, 90 min then 10x (50 °C 2 min / 42 °C 2 min). The RT reaches the mRNA 5' end, adds non-templated Cs, and the rGrGrG of the TSO base-pairs, templating incorporation of the TSO 5' handle (tag + UMI). Improved TSO adds a WW spacer to suppress strand invasion.
library_generation.product:
5'- ACGAGCATCAGCAGCATACGA(T)30VN-[cDNA]-CCC-[UMI']-CATTGCGCAATGTCTCTCT -3'
3'- ...(A)n...................[mRNA/cDNA]...GGG-[UMI ]-GTAACGCGTTAC... -5'
  (first-strand cDNA now flanked by dT handle (3') and TSO tag+UMI (5'))

library_generation.title: Pre-amplification PCR
library_generation.summary: Forward primer (s5/ME + tag) and reverse primer (dT handle) amplify full-length cDNA for 10-16 cycles.
library_generation.note: SeqAmp (preferred) or KAPA HiFi at 0.5-1 uM each primer. Forward primer installs the Nextera s5+ME handle upstream of the tag; only low cycle numbers are used so no QC/normalization is required.

library_generation.title: Dilution (no cleanup)
library_generation.summary: Pre-amplified cDNA is diluted ~1:10 in water with no bead cleanup, QC or normalization.
library_generation.note: Typically +9 µl water; 1 µl of diluted cDNA is carried into tagmentation. Skipping cleanup/QC is the key time/cost saving of Smart-seq3xpress.
library_generation.product: (diluted double-stranded cDNA, unchanged structure from step 3)

library_generation.title: Tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Tn5 (TDE1/ATM or in-house) fragments the cDNA and inserts mosaic-end (ME) adapters.
library_generation.note: 55 °C, 10 min, then 0.2% SDS to strip Tn5. Amount of Tn5 tunes the ratio of UMI-containing 5' reads (few cuts) to internal full-transcript reads (more cuts). The 5'-most fragment retains the s5-tag-UMI-GGG block.
library_generation.product:
5'-fragment:   TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-ATTGCGCAATG[UMI:8]GGG-[CDNA]-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT
internal frag: ...AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-[CDNA]-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT...  (ME at both new cut sites)

library_generation.title: Dual-index PCR
library_generation.summary: Custom Nextera i5/i7 index primers add P5+i5 and P7+i7 with 10-bp dual indexes.
library_generation.note: Phusion polymerase, 3 min 72 °C gap-fill then 12-14 cycles. The i5 primer extends the s5 end to P5; the i7 primer binds the tagmented ME/s7 end to add i7+P7.

library_generation.title: Pooling & purification
library_generation.summary: Plates are pooled by gentle centrifugation into a reservoir and purified with 22% PEG beads (1:0.7).
library_generation.note: A 3D-printed adapter spins the 384-well plate contents into a Nalgene reservoir (~200 g), avoiding manual pooling. Final library is sequencing-ready.
library_generation.product: (purified pooled dual-index library, structure as in step 6)

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 primer (Nextera s5 + ME) anneals to the bottom strand; the polymerase reads the top strand 5'->3'. For 5'-UMI molecules it reads the 11-nt tag ATTGCGCAATG + 8-nt UMI + GGG, then cDNA; internal-fragment reads start directly in cDNA. Minimum supported read length is not below 75 bases because of the tag+UMI+GGG. zUMIs parses UMI reads via the ATTGCGCAATG pattern (≤2 mismatches).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (s5+ME) anneals to the bottom strand; reads the top strand 5'->3':
        5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------------>
5'- [i5:10]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGATTGCGCAATG[UMI:8]GGG[CDNA]... -3'
3'- [i5:10]AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTCTAACGCGTTAC[UMI:8]CCC[CDNA]... -5'
                                             |___tag___| UMI  GGG   cDNA

library_sequencing.note: 10-bp i7 (index1) of the custom Nextera dual-index pair. The i7 primer anneals to the top strand at the P7 site and reads the i7 index off the top-strand template.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer (P7) anneals to the top strand; reads the i7 index (template = top):
                          [i7 primer, reads i7]<------------
5'- ...CCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:10]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...GGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:10]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                       |i7|  |______P7 revcomp______|

library_sequencing.note: 10-bp i5 (index2) of the custom Nextera dual-index pair. The i5 primer anneals to the top strand at the s5+ME site and reads the i5 index off the bottom-strand template (as on NextSeq/DNBSEQ).
library_sequencing.diagram:
i5 index primer (s5+ME revcomp) anneals to the top strand; reads the i5 index (template = bottom):
                              [i5 primer, reads i5]<------------
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5:10]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG... -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[i5:10]AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC... -5'
    |__________P5__________|      |i5|  |________s5 + ME________|

library_sequencing.note: Read 2 primer (Nextera s7 + ME) anneals to the top strand at the tagmented (i7) end and reads internal cDNA off the bottom-strand template. Paired-end sequencing is needed for molecule reconstruction / full-transcript coverage; single-end suffices for gene counting. Libraries run on Illumina (NextSeq 500) and MGI DNBSEQ G400RS.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 primer (s7+ME) anneals to the top strand; reads internal cDNA toward the 5' end (template = bottom):
                    <------------GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5' [Read 2 primer, 3'->5']
5'- ...[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:10]... -3'
3'- ...[CDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:10]... -5'
            |____ME revcomp____|_s7 revcomp__|

title: Smart-seq3xpress
description: Smart-seq3xpress is a miniaturized, streamlined plate-based full-length single-cell RNA-seq method — a nanoliter implementation of Smart-seq3. Single cells are FACS-sorted into ~300 nl of lysis buffer under a hydrophobic overlay; an oligo-dT primer primes reverse transcription while a biotinylated template-switching oligo (TSO) appends a 5' recognition tag, an 8-nt UMI and a GGG at the transcript 5' end. Low-cycle pre-amplification (SeqAmp/KAPA) with a tagged forward primer is followed — without cleanup or QC, only 1:10 dilution — by Tn5 tagmentation and custom dual-index Nextera PCR to make sequencing-ready libraries in one workday. 5' reads carry the UMI for accurate molecule counting; internal tagmentation reads give full-transcript coverage enabling isoform-, splicing- and allele-specific analysis.

original_publication.title: Scalable single-cell RNA sequencing from full transcripts with Smart-seq3xpress

publication.statistical_model: Error-corrected UMI-based molecule counting (validated with molecular spikes); Bayesian regression (BRIE2) used for isoform PSI / differential-splicing inference downstream.

authors.name: Michael Hagemann-Jensen
authors.affiliation: Department of Cell and Molecular Biology, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm, Sweden

authors.name: Christoph Ziegenhain
authors.affiliation: Department of Cell and Molecular Biology, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm, Sweden

authors.name: Rickard Sandberg
authors.affiliation: Department of Cell and Molecular Biology, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm, Sweden

throughput.summary: Plate-based, one cell per well; throughput limited only by available equipment (PCR instruments). Demonstrated on 26,260 human PBMCs at an average depth of 258,000 read pairs per cell; ~10-fold reduced reagents/cost vs Smart-seq3, sequencing-ready libraries in a single workday.
throughput.rna: Full-transcript coverage with 5' UMI counting; ~9.2 million transcribed SNP positions covered vs 2.1 million for matched 10x 3' v3.1
throughput.dna: Not applicable (RNA assay; no genomic DNA library)

other.label: Lysis volume
other.value: ~300 nl under 3 µl hydrophobic overlay (e.g. Vapor-Lock)

other.label: Preferred PCR polymerase
other.value: SeqAmp (replaces KAPA to enable direct tagmentation)

other.label: Tagmentation enzyme
other.value: TDE1 (or ATM) Tn5; amount tunes 5'-UMI vs internal read ratio

other.label: Strand-invasion fix
other.value: Improved TSO adds a 2-nt WW (A/T) spacer between UMI and rGrGrG

other.label: Sequencers
other.value: Illumina NextSeq 500 and MGI DNBSEQ G400RS (min read length ≥75)

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed and UMI-parsed with zUMIs — 5' UMI reads are identified by the ATTGCGCAATG recognition tag (allowing ≤2 mismatches) — aligned to the human genome (hg38) with STAR, and error-corrected UMI counts are tabulated from Ensembl GRCh38.95 gene annotations. TSO strand-invasion artifact reads are removed with pyTSOfilter before downstream clustering and isoform/allele analyses.
data_processing.statistical_model: Error-corrected UMI-based molecule counting (validated with molecular spikes); BRIE2 Bayesian regression for differential-splicing PSI inference
